commentaries.author: Clement Of Rome
commentaries.content:
"For whom the Lord loveth He chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.".
"For whom the Lord loveth He chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom He receiveth."

commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: For know, beloved brethren, that I was not long ago reproached with this also in a vision, that we were sleepy in our prayers, and did not pray with watchfulness; and undoubtedly God, who "rebukes whom He loves,

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: You can not say that any righteous man is without affliction: even if he appear to be so, yet we know not his other afflictions. So that of necessity every righteous man must pass through affliction. For it is a declaration of Christ, that the wide and broad way leads to destruction, but the strait and narrow one to life. Matthew 7:13-14 If then it is possible to enter into life by that means, and is not by any other, then all have entered in by the narrow [way], as many as have departed unto life.
